theme_and_archetype.rationale: Patients need warmth, calm, trust. Dark UI feels clinical/cold. Low-arousal design demands muted colors, reduced contrast, and a grounded feel.

rules: NO pure white (#FFFFFF) in Child Mode. It creates high contrast that can cause visual stress.
NO pure black (#000000). Use tinted dark olives.
NO bright neon colors, gradients, or high contrast palettes.
NO shadows in Child Mode. Use soft borders or subtle color blocking.

sizing.child_mode_h1: text-4xl (36px) - Maximum size for primary task text

rules: High legibility is critical. Do not use ultra-thin or ultra-black weights.
Avoid ALL CAPS as it reduces word shape recognition for autistic children.

child_mode.strategy: Absolute Focus. Max 1-2 interactive elements per screen.
child_mode.padding: Generous spacing. p-8 to p-12 (32px - 48px) safe areas.
child_mode.touch_targets: MINIMUM 88x88 points for all interactive elements.
child_mode.alignment: Center-aligned for single focus tasks, left-aligned for Choice Boards.
child_mode.border_radius: rounded-3xl (24px) to soften UI and make it friendly.

admin_mode.strategy: Control Room Grid. High density, functional.

child_task_card.layout: Column layout: Large photo top (max-h 50vh), Short text below, giant 'Done' checkmark button at bottom.
child_task_card.visuals: No shadows. Use subtle background contrast. Real object photos.

choice_board.layout: Row or Wrap layout: 2-3 large square cards.
choice_board.visuals: Muted background, large photo, one-word label.

sos_button.layout: Fixed position (e.g., top-right). Persistent across Child Mode screens.
sos_button.visuals: Muted terracotta (#D98A6C), clearly labeled 'Help', Lucide 'LifeBuoy' or 'Hand' icon.

parental_gate.trigger: Settings Gear Icon -> Long press 3 seconds.
parental_gate.visuals: Numeric PIN pad (3x4 grid), large touch areas, minimal styling.

admin_routine_builder.visuals: Cardless design, 1px border separation, left-aligned text, right-aligned drag handle.

rules: Icons must be literal and straightforward (e.g., Checkmark for done, Gear for settings).
Include text labels with icons where possible.
NO timers or progress bars that imply urgency.
Use only subtle opacity fades (opacity 0 to 1 over 300ms) for screen transitions.

testing.attributes: Use 'testID' (React Native) for all interactive and key informational elements. Format: kebab-case, role-based. e.g., 'task-done-button', 'sos-help-button', 'pin-pad-digit-1'.

morning_routine_toothbrush.description: Bamboo toothbrush with water running. Use as default image for 'Morning: Brush Teeth' task.

morning_routine_breakfast.description: Cereal bowl sitting on top of a wooden table. Use as default image for 'Morning: Breakfast' task.

after_school_shoes.description: Child wearing red pants and black shoes indoors. Crop to shoes. Use as default image for 'After-school: Take off shoes' task.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build the app using React Native and Expo.
Ensure all code is written in .js files as requested.
Implement the UI directly using React Native StyleSheet or NativeWind/Tailwind depending on setup, strictly following the colors and spacing here.
For the Parental Gate, implement a LongPressGestureHandler or TouchableOpacity with delayLongPress={3000}.
Ensure Child Mode cannot easily be exited via native back gestures without hitting the PIN pad.
Do NOT use pure white or pure black anywhere in Child Mode.
Do not add countdown timers to Choice Boards or Tasks.
